articles.content:
Alexander Lambridis, a 22-year-old Florida Atlantic University political science student, did not qualify for the Florida House District 91 race after a discrepancy on his qualifying check. Election records show the numeric amount on the check matched the required $1,781.82 fee, but the written amount listed $1,780.82.

Lambridis said he and his lawyer are still trying to get him on the ballot, but said he viewed the situation as a learning experience for a first-time candidate. He announced his campaign in April and had focused on housing, insurance, and affordability issues.

District 91 includes parts of Palm Beach County including Boca Raton and FAU. Republican Rep. Peggy Gossett-Seidman currently holds the seat. Democrat Luis Garcia has also qualified to run. The article noted that other Florida candidates have previously challenged ballot disqualifications tied to technical errors.

Senate Democratic Leader Lori Berman said the c
articles.content:
A Florida Politics roundup reported that the DeSantis administration has begun dismantling the Everglades immigration detention center known as “Alligator Alcatraz.” According to the report, contractors were told to begin full demobilization after remaining detainees were transferred out last week, and Florida Division of Emergency Management Director Kevin Guthrie told vendors to wind down the site with significant progress expected by Wednesday.

Senate Democratic Leader Lori Berman said the closure supports concerns Democrats had raised about the facility’s cost, environmental impact, and treatment of detainees. Attorney General James Uthmeier said the site was no longer needed because the federal government now has the resources to carry out its own mission, and he said the long-term goal is for the property to return to protected Everglades land.

The digest also included links to national and state political coverage, a quote from Uthmeier about banned 7-OH products, campaign notes from Florida races, and a preview of the Tampa Bay Rays’ series opener against the Kansas City Royals.

articles.content:
The National Park Service began draining the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool on Monday as crews worked to address algae growth and peeling paint following a recent renovation. A contractor told E&E News the repairs were not expected to be finished by July 4.

Workers at the site were seen vacuuming algae from the water while equipment described by the administration as nanobubble ozone technology operated nearby. Park Police, U.S. Marshals, National Guard personnel, and other officers were also present around the pool during the weekend.

The administration said Monday that Park Police had made five arrests and issued five citations related to alleged vandalism. President Donald Trump said vandalism caused some of the damage, while the article reported no publicly presented evidence linking vandalism to the algae growth. The report also noted that heat, shallow water, and filtration problems can contribute to algae blooms.

Board of Governors Chair Alan Levine has delayed confirmation
articles.content:
The University of Florida Board of Trustees voted Monday to select Stuart Bell as interim president while a dispute continues with the State University System Board of Governors over the hiring process. Bell’s contract includes a $2 million salary, a $500,000 termination payment if he is released from the contract, and about 20 weeks of base salary if he completes the interim term through the end of June and is not hired permanently.

Board of Governors Chair Alan Levine has delayed confirmation of Bell and raised concerns about the role of UF Board Chair Mori Hosseini in the search process. During the trustees meeting, Hosseini denied wrongdoing. Other trustees rejected calls for an investigation based on anonymous allegations. Levine said UF and the Board of Governors had agreed to bring in a third-party expert to review governance documents, delegated authorities, and board policies across the university system.

Bell would become the third interim UF president since Ben Sasse was the last permanent president. Trustees said they support Bell and cited the upcoming departure of current interim leader Donald W. Landry, who is returning to Columbia University on July 1. Bell’s past positions on diversity, equity, and inclusion have also drawn scrutiny from some conservative figures, while other Florida Republican officials have defended the selection.

articles.content:
GTA 6 preorders go live on June 25, and ahead of that, one retailer posted listings for the game showing higher-than-expected price points. A prominent and reliable reporter, however, has said those were just placeholders.

The retailer fnac listed GTA 6's standard edition at €89.99, which is about $103 USD. The listings scaled all the way up to €199.99 ($230 USD). However, billbil-kun of the magazine Dealabs said these listings are just placeholders. 

"So those prices are random ones," they wrote. 

Many are expecting GTA 6's price for the standard edition to be more than $70, but that remains to be seen. In any event, $80 games are already here. 

GTA 6 preorders will go live on June 25 for PS5 and Xbox Series X|S, so the long wait to find out how much the game will cost will soon be over. In addition to a standard edition, it's expected there will be premium versions people can buy. Many are also theorizing that Rockstar will release a new GTA 6 trailer this week to get people even more hyped up and encourage them to preorder the game.

GTA 6 launches on November 19 for PS5 and Xbox Series X|S. A PC edition of the game has not been announced, but history suggests it is coming ... eventually.

articles.content:
A UBS tokenized money-market fund is being used as collateral on Bybit in an arrangement involving ByCustody and DigiFT, according to the source article. The setup allows a position in UBS uMINT to remain in custody while still being recognized as exchange collateral.

The article said Calais, a Singapore-based quantitative investment fund, is using the product in active trading operations through an off-exchange settlement structure. DigiFT provides access and distribution for uMINT, ByCustody holds the asset, and Bybit accepts the custodied position as collateral.

According to the source, the arrangement is intended to let trading firms use a yield-bearing money-market product instead of leaving margin in idle cash or similar assets. The article described that as a step toward making tokenized real-world assets more useful in day-to-day trading infrastructure rather than limiting them to issuance and holding.

UBS launched uMINT in November 2024 as a tokenized money-market investment fund on Ethereum, according to the article. The source said the product is managed by UBS Asset Management (Singapore) Ltd. and is designed to provide access to cash-management exposure backed by high-quality money-market instruments.

The article cited asset-page data showing that, as of June 21, uMINT had about $18.7 million in total asset value, 176,116 tokens, and 29 holders. The source characterized that scale as early but large enough to demonstrate a live institutional use case.

The article also said several operational details remain unclear, including collateral haircuts, valuation methods, marking frequency, liquidation procedures, and the interaction between exchange risk systems and fund redemption timing. It said those terms will be important in assessing whether the model can expand beyond a limited institutional arrangement.

According to DigiFT materials cited in the article, the product and related services are available through authorized and regulated intermediaries to eligible investors. The source said that suggests adoption is likely to remain focused on professional and institutional clients unless access rules change.

AllUnity said it launched SEKAU on June 19 as a Swedish krona-backed stablecoin under the European Union’s MiCA framework. The company said the token is fully reserved, redeemable 1:1 for SEK, and available on Ethereum, Solana, Base, Tempo, and Polygon.

According to AllUnity, SEKAU is designed for treasury, settlement, and business payment use cases. The company said the token is intended primarily for business and institutional clients and that access is tied to onboarding and linked corporate
articles.content:
AllUnity said it launched SEKAU on June 19 as a Swedish krona-backed stablecoin under the European Union’s MiCA framework. The company said the token is fully reserved, redeemable 1:1 for SEK, and available on Ethereum, Solana, Base, Tempo, and Polygon.

According to AllUnity, SEKAU is designed for treasury, settlement, and business payment use cases. The company said the token is intended primarily for business and institutional clients and that access is tied to onboarding and linked corporate bank accounts and wallets.

The launch adds a krona-denominated product to AllUnity’s existing multi-currency strategy, which also includes euro- and Swiss franc-based offerings. AllUnity said Banking Circle is the reserve and transaction bank for SEKAU and that Marginalen Bank is also supporting the product.

The launch comes as dollar-backed stablecoins continue to dominate on-chain payments and trading. That means SEKAU’s adoption will depend less on its technical rollout and regulatory structure than on whether institutions choose to use SEK-denominated liquidity in blockchain-based workflows.

Public disclosures reviewed in the source article did not identify major public trading venues, retail minting access, or post-launch circulation and transaction data. The article said those measures will be important in determining whether the token gains meaningful use beyond its initial launch.

The source article also noted that Sweden already has domestic instant-payment infrastructure, which may limit the token’s value for ordinary local payments. Its potential use cases may instead center on corporate treasury operations, tokenized assets, regional settlement, and other blockchain-based financial activity that requires SEK exposure.

articles.content:
Microsoft said a malware campaign known as CryptoBandits is targeting cryptocurrency wallet activity on Windows computers. According to the article, the malware can alter copied wallet addresses, capture seed phrases before transactions are signed and send screenshots or wallet information to attackers. It can also use connected USB drives as part of the attack path.

The report presents the campaign as a security risk for users who manage crypto assets on compromised personal devices. The article says wallet theft can occur before an on-chain transaction is finalized, including through clipboard hijacking and data exfiltration.

Microsoft's warning focuses on endpoint security rather than a flaw in a specific blockchain. The article says the case highlights operational risks in self-custody when attackers gain access to a user's computer.

articles.content:
Bitwise Asset Management said it is adding its crypto model portfolios to the retail investing app from Parrot Finance. The June 18 partnership gives Parrot clients access to portfolios built around broad crypto exposure, thematic strategies, systematic monitoring and rebalancing. Bitwise said Parrot clients collectively hold more than $200 million on the platform, but the companies did not disclose expected allocations or projected flows.

Bitwise's lineup includes broad crypto portfolios, crypto-equity strategies, thematic portfolios and a risk-managed strategy that rotates between bitcoin and ether futures exposure and U.S. Treasuries.

The article describes the rollout as a distribution expansion rather than evidence of new investor inflows. It says the next indicator will be whether Parrot later reports meaningful user adoption of the model portfolios.

The article notes that some users may view tokens shown in a major exchange app as vetted even though Kraken says they remain outside its review process. It also says the feature currently relies on Solana and USDC as the underlying trading rails.

articles.summary:
Ethereum contributors are debating a proposal that would let validators direct a portion of staking rewards to ecosystem development and public goods. Under the plan, validators would signal a contribution rate, and if 51% backed a non-zero deduction, it would apply across the validator set. The proposal caps the redirect at 10%. Based on the article's figures, that could amount to about 70,000 ETH annually, or roughly $120 million at cited market prices.

Supporters say the mechanism could prov
articles.content:
Ethereum contributors are debating a proposal that would let validators direct a portion of staking rewards to ecosystem development and public goods. Under the plan, validators would signal a contribution rate, and if 51% backed a non-zero deduction, it would apply across the validator set. The proposal caps the redirect at 10%. Based on the article's figures, that could amount to about 70,000 ETH annually, or roughly $120 million at cited market prices.

Supporters say the mechanism could provide recurring funding for open-source security tools, client upgrades and other shared infrastructure. Critics, including attorney Gabriel Shapiro and developer Lefteris Karapetsas, raised concerns about governance, incentives and whether large staking providers could shape funding decisions for the rest of the network. Some critics also said exchange and staking-service customers could bear lower rewards even though operators would cast the votes.

The debate comes as the Ethereum Foundation reduces its size and focus. The article says former contributor Trent Van Epps warned of a possible development funding shortfall within three to nine months, while other industry figures, including Thomas Lee and Joseph Lubin, said private capital could help fill any gap. The proposal has not been adopted, and Ethereum's long-term funding approach remains unresolved.

articles.headline: Ethereum funding proposal draws criticism over validator control
articles.deck: Lefteris Karapetsas, founder of Rotki and an Ethereum developer, said he opposes a research proposal that would allow validators to redirect 0% to 10% of staking rewards to publ...
articles.summary:
Lefteris Karapetsas, founder of Rotki and an Ethereum developer, said he opposes a research proposal that would allow validators to redirect 0% to 10% of staking rewards to public goods, infrastructure and core development. Under the proposal, a non-zero contribution would apply across the validator set if more than half of validators supported it.

Karapetsas said the design could give the largest staking operators too much influence over funding decisions and could require other validators to
articles.content:
Lefteris Karapetsas, founder of Rotki and an Ethereum developer, said he opposes a research proposal that would allow validators to redirect 0% to 10% of staking rewards to public goods, infrastructure and core development. Under the proposal, a non-zero contribution would apply across the validator set if more than half of validators supported it.

Karapetsas said the design could give the largest staking operators too much influence over funding decisions and could require other validators to support allocations they did not choose. Supporters say the mechanism is intended to address a funding gap for shared Ethereum infrastructure. Earlier reporting cited estimates that a 5% to 10% redirect could raise about 50,000 to 70,000 ETH per year.

Karapetsas also said Ethereum core development has become too complex and too removed from protocol users, arguing that tighter funding could force consolidation. He said that, if a funding mechanism is needed, he would prefer using burned ETH fees rather than validator rewards. The proposal remains at the research stage and is not an active network rule change.

articles.content:
Crypto.news published a sponsored article describing eCurrency as a Layer-1 blockchain designed for digital payments. The project says the network has operated since 2018 and uses a fixed supply of 333,333,333 ECR tokens.

According to the article, the network uses a UTXO-native proof-of-stake system with 10-second block intervals and a maximum of 65,535 transactions per block. It says validators can participate without capital lockups or slashing, and that rewards are distributed through a protocol-level fund.

The article also says eCurrency supports client-side smart contracts and uses Falcon, a post-quantum digital signature scheme selected by the U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology in its standardization process. It adds that the protocol supports other signature methods and is designed to allow future cryptographic upgrades.

The sponsored piece says ECR is used for transaction processing and validator participation and does not provide ownership, governance, or profit rights. It also states that exchange listing discussions are underway.

articles.content:

articles.headline: Guide Explains How Perpetual Futures Work in Crypto Markets
articles.deck: Perpetual futures are derivative contracts that let traders take long or short positions with leverage without a set expiration date, according to the source article.
articles.summary:
Perpetual futures are derivative contracts that let traders take long or short positions with leverage without a set expiration date, according to the source article. The piece says these contracts are designed to track the spot price of an asset through a funding-rate system in which payments move between long and short traders.

The article explains that when perpetual futures trade above spot prices, longs typically pay shorts, and when they trade below spot, shorts pay longs. It says this me
articles.content:
Perpetual futures are derivative contracts that let traders take long or short positions with leverage without a set expiration date, according to the source article. The piece says these contracts are designed to track the spot price of an asset through a funding-rate system in which payments move between long and short traders.

The article explains that when perpetual futures trade above spot prices, longs typically pay shorts, and when they trade below spot, shorts pay longs. It says this mechanism helps keep the contract price aligned with the underlying market even though the contract does not expire.

The source also describes how leverage increases both potential gains and losses. Traders post margin as collateral, and positions can be liquidated automatically if losses consume that margin. The article notes that liquidation is usually based on a mark price derived from broader market data rather than only the last traded price on one venue.

It further says perpetual futures became widely used in crypto after BitMEX introduced the perpetual swap in 2016. The article lists centralized exchanges such as Binance, Bybit, OKX, Deribit, and BitMEX, as well as on-chain venues including Hyperliquid, dYdX, and GMX, as places where such products trade.

According to the source, the U.S. regulatory environment began shifting in 2026 as crypto perpetual products started moving into regulated domestic markets. The article also says debate continues over how these products should be classified and regulated.

The piece characterizes perpetual futures as high-risk instruments because leverage, funding costs, liquidation cascades, and platform risk can quickly increase losses.

Venus Protocol has added markets for tokenized stock products to its Core Pool on BNB Chain, allowing eligible users to supply those assets as collateral while borrowing remains disabled at launch. The June 20 rollout covers TSLAB, NVDAB, and SPCXB, which provide exposure linked to Tesla, Nvidia, and SpaceX.

According to the launch parameters described in the source material, TSLAB and NVDAB were assigned 60% collateral factors and SPCXB a 50% collateral factor. Borrow caps for the markets were
articles.content:
Venus Protocol has added markets for tokenized stock products to its Core Pool on BNB Chain, allowing eligible users to supply those assets as collateral while borrowing remains disabled at launch. The June 20 rollout covers TSLAB, NVDAB, and SPCXB, which provide exposure linked to Tesla, Nvidia, and SpaceX.

According to the launch parameters described in the source material, TSLAB and NVDAB were assigned 60% collateral factors and SPCXB a 50% collateral factor. Borrow caps for the markets were set at zero at launch, indicating that the initial rollout is limited to testing collateral use rather than active borrowing.

The article says the structure is intended to test whether stock-linked tokens can function inside a DeFi lending market while managing pricing, liquidation, and liquidity risks. It notes that tokenized equities introduce additional considerations beyond typical crypto collateral, including issuer controls, jurisdictional limits, oracle design, and differences between round-the-clock crypto trading and the underlying equity market.

The source also states that Binance describes bStocks as 1:1-backed tokenized securities for eligible users in permitted jurisdictions, with BTech Holdings Limited identified as issuer in Binance product materials. The tokens represent stock-linked exposure rather than direct ownership of Tesla, Nvidia, or SpaceX shares.

Venus’ rollout places the assets into a live lending framework, but the source says key questions remain unresolved, including whether borrowing will later be enabled, whether collateral supply will develop without incentives, and whether pricing and liquidation systems will function reliably for equity-linked products.

articles.content:
MoneyGram said on June 22 that it has become a validator on the Solana blockchain and joined the Solana Developer Platform, expanding its use of blockchain infrastructure in its payments business.

According to the announcement, the company now operates an active validator on Solana, where it stakes SOL, processes transaction blocks, and contributes to network operations. MoneyGram also said it has joined the Solana Developer Platform, which it described as a development environment for institutions building financial products on Solana.

Luke Tuttle, MoneyGram’s chief product and technology officer, said operating a validator places the company directly in Solana’s consensus process. Sheraz Shere, general manager of payments and commerce at the Solana Foundation, said the move shows how payment companies are becoming more directly involved in blockchain networks.

MoneyGram said it joined the Solana Developer Platform alongside institutions including Mastercard. Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Anthony Soohoo said blockchain infrastructure has become a core part of the company’s payment systems and future development plans.

The company did not announce a new Solana-based payment product. The article said the move follows the June 2 launch of MGUSD, MoneyGram’s U.S. dollar stablecoin on Stellar, through partnerships with Bridge, M0, and Fireblocks. It also said MoneyGram previously became a validator on Tempo and Midnight, making Solana the third blockchain network where it operates an official validator.

articles.content:
Strategy said in a June 22 filing that it bought 520 Bitcoin for about $35 million at an average price of $67,068 per coin, bringing its total holdings to 847,363 BTC.

The filing also showed that the company increased its cash reserves by about $300 million to $1.4 billion after selling 2.71 million MSTR shares for roughly $335.5 million during the prior week. According to the report, only a small portion of the share-sale proceeds appears to have been used for the latest Bitcoin purchase, with most retained as cash.

Strategy said it plans to continue replenishing its U.S. dollar reserve to support the credit quality of its Digital Credit securities. The disclosure came as investors continued to watch STRC, the company’s preferred stock, which the article said had recently traded below its $100 par value.

The report said some investors expect the company to raise STRC’s dividend rate or consider stock buybacks. Executive Chairman Michael Saylor, according to comments cited in the article, said Strategy’s combined Bitcoin and cash holdings exceed its outstanding debt by about $48 billion and that the company has raised more than $60 billion in capital since 2022 for Bitcoin purchases.

The article also cited criticism from Peter Schiff, who said investors could eventually pursue legal action against Strategy and Saylor and alleged STRC may have been promoted in violation of SEC marketing rules. The report said no regulatory findings supporting that allegation had been announced. It also cited Arca Chief Investment Officer Jeff Dorman, who previously suggested Strategy might need to sell between $3 billion and $4 billion in Bitcoin to reduce pressure on its capital structure and support STRC holders.

According to the report, MSTR shares rose 3.44% to $116.40 in pre-market trading on June 22 after Saylor confirmed the latest Bitcoin purchase.

articles.content:
BingX said it has launched Ultra TradingView, a new version of its charting and trading interface designed for professional users. The company announced the release from Panama City on June 18.

According to the announcement, the upgrade adds chart-based order management, including the ability to modify orders, set take-profit and stop-loss levels, reverse positions, and place orders without leaving the chart view. BingX also said the product includes real-time order labels, 86 drawing tools, 108 technical indicators, and 16 candlestick styles.

The company said users can customize order-confirmation prompts and that the updated interface supports chart-based execution in the BingX mobile app. BingX described itself as the first major exchange to offer in-chart execution on mobile.

BingX said it will run a promotional campaign from June 18 through July 1, 2026, with rewards of up to $2,000 for participants who use the new features and share their experiences.

In background information included with the announcement, BingX said it was founded in 2018, serves more than 40 million users, and offers products including futures, spot trading, copy trading, and other services.

articles.content:
Bitcoin rose back above $65,000 on June 22 after rebounding from the low-$63,000 range, while crude oil prices fell to about $73 per barrel, down 4.49% on the day. CryptoSlate reported that Bitcoin briefly reached about $65,500 before easing slightly below $65,000.

The article said lower oil prices may reduce near-term inflation concerns that had weighed on risk assets during recent Middle East tensions. At the same time, the U.S. Dollar Index remained near 101 and the 10-year Treasury yield held around 4.5%, conditions the report said can limit demand for more volatile assets.

CryptoSlate reported that Bitcoin moved from $63,231 to $65,442 over 24 hours and that the asset had a market capitalization of about $1.31 trillion with $23.23 billion in 24-hour trading volume. The report said Bitcoin remained lower over the previous seven-day and 30-day periods.

According to the article, traders are watching whether Bitcoin can hold the $65,000 to $66,000 range while the dollar and Treasury yields remain firm. It said stronger confirmation of the rebound would include Bitcoin holding that range while the dollar and yields move lower.

articles.headline: Strategy Uses Most Recent Share Sale to Increase Cash Reserve Rather Than Buy Bitcoin

articles.summary:
Strategy raised about $335.5 million by selling roughly 2.71 million MSTR shares between June 15 and June 21, then directed about $300 million of the proceeds to its U.S. dollar reserve and about $34.9 million to the purchase of 520 Bitcoin.

The company increased its cash reserve to $1.4 billion after STRC perpetual preferred shares fell below their $100 stated value. Because Strategy typically issues new STRC shares when they trade at or above par, the decline reduced the usefulness of that fu
articles.content:
Strategy raised about $335.5 million by selling roughly 2.71 million MSTR shares between June 15 and June 21, then directed about $300 million of the proceeds to its U.S. dollar reserve and about $34.9 million to the purchase of 520 Bitcoin.

The company increased its cash reserve to $1.4 billion after STRC perpetual preferred shares fell below their $100 stated value. Because Strategy typically issues new STRC shares when they trade at or above par, the decline reduced the usefulness of that funding route for additional Bitcoin purchases.

The article said the move strengthened liquidity for dividends and other obligations across the company's capital structure, but also diluted common shareholders and reduced the pace of Bitcoin accumulation compared with earlier purchases.

A filing by 24X National Exchange would allow eligible members to trade certain securities in tokenized form during a Depository Trust Company pilot, while keeping trading, shareholder rights, clearing and settlement within the existing U.S. market system.

articles.content:
A filing by 24X National Exchange would allow eligible members to trade certain securities in tokenized form during a Depository Trust Company pilot, while keeping trading, shareholder rights, clearing and settlement within the existing U.S. market system.

According to the article, the proposal would apply to securities that remain fungible with their traditional form, share the same CUSIP and trading symbol, and provide the same rights and privileges. Participants seeking tokenized settlement would need to meet exchange and DTC eligibility requirements, including approved wallets and compatible blockchain infrastructure.

The article said the model treats tokenization as a new representation layer rather than a separate parallel market. DTC would continue to control the official recordkeeping and settlement framework during the pilot.

articles.headline: Bank of England Sets Initial £40 Billion Cap for Systemic Sterling Stablecoins
articles.deck: The Bank of England said it will remove proposed holding limits for individuals and businesses using systemic sterling stablecoins, but plans to impose an initial £40 billion is...
articles.summary:
The Bank of England said it will remove proposed holding limits for individuals and businesses using systemic sterling stablecoins, but plans to impose an initial £40 billion issuance cap on each systemic product.

In a June 22 policy statement and draft code of practice, the bank also said issuers may hold up to 70% of reserve assets in eligible short-term government securities, with the remaining 30% held in central bank deposits. The consultation closes Sept. 22, 2026, and the framework is in
articles.content:
The Bank of England said it will remove proposed holding limits for individuals and businesses using systemic sterling stablecoins, but plans to impose an initial £40 billion issuance cap on each systemic product.

In a June 22 policy statement and draft code of practice, the bank also said issuers may hold up to 70% of reserve assets in eligible short-term government securities, with the remaining 30% held in central bank deposits. The consultation closes Sept. 22, 2026, and the framework is intended to support regulated operations beginning in 2027.

The bank described the issuance cap as a temporary financial-stability measure intended to limit the risk of large shifts out of bank deposits. The article noted that the approach removes wallet-level restrictions but still limits the scale any single pound-denominated stablecoin can reach at launch.

articles.headline: Altura Begins Orderly Wind-Down After More Than $8.5 Million in Redemptions
articles.deck: Altura said users redeemed more than $8.5 million in USDT from one of its vaults in 24 hours, prompting the team to begin an orderly wind-down.
articles.summary:
Altura said users redeemed more than $8.5 million in USDT from one of its vaults in 24 hours, prompting the team to begin an orderly wind-down. The withdrawals followed a broader loss of confidence in some yield-bearing stablecoin products after Accountable ended a verification relationship with MainStreet.

Altura said it had no direct exposure to MainStreet or its underlying strategies and said its HyperEVM lending vault, related USDT/AVLT market and Ethereum-vault borrowers were unaffected. E
articles.content:
Altura said users redeemed more than $8.5 million in USDT from one of its vaults in 24 hours, prompting the team to begin an orderly wind-down. The withdrawals followed a broader loss of confidence in some yield-bearing stablecoin products after Accountable ended a verification relationship with MainStreet.

Altura said it had no direct exposure to MainStreet or its underlying strategies and said its HyperEVM lending vault, related USDT/AVLT market and Ethereum-vault borrowers were unaffected. Even so, the episode highlighted how verification disputes at one firm can affect confidence in similar products.

The article said the main issue was liquidity under stress rather than confirmed asset losses. It noted that users may expect immediate withdrawals even when underlying strategies rely on exchanges, private credit or real-world asset positions that settle on different timelines.

articles.content:
Ark Invest bought about 210,121 shares of SpaceX across four exchange-traded funds, according to the firm's daily trading disclosure for June 22. Based on the stock's closing price of $154.60, the purchase was worth about $32.5 million.

The purchase followed a decline of more than 16% from the stock's recent intraday high after its June 12 public listing. The article said SpaceX had become one of Ark's larger holdings, including a 4.46% weighting in the ARK Innovation ETF.

The new purchase followed a much larger SpaceX investment by Ark on the company's first trading day. The article also noted that SpaceX announced its first bond offering and said the proceeds would be used to repay bridge financing and for general corporate purposes.

articles.content:
Securitize has filed a complaint in the U.S. District Court for the District of Delaware seeking a declaratory judgment that its products do not infringe patents asserted by tZERO. The case follows a cease-and-desist letter from tZERO.

tZERO said Securitize's DS Protocol and Vault Registrar infringe patents related to compliance controls and crypto integration in tokenized securities infrastructure. Securitize said the claims are without merit and said it will continue defending its position in court.

The dispute comes as interest in tokenized securities continues to grow among financial firms. According to the article, Securitize is also preparing for a shareholder vote on its planned merger with Cantor Equity Partners II, a step that could lead to a New York Stock Exchange listing.

articles.headline: China Signals Stronger Anti-Money Laundering Enforcement Involving Virtual Currencies
articles.deck: The People's Bank of China said authorities will increase enforcement against money laundering involving virtual currencies, underground banks and cross-border fund transfers as...
articles.summary:
The People's Bank of China said authorities will increase enforcement against money laundering involving virtual currencies, underground banks and cross-border fund transfers as part of the country's anti-money laundering framework.

In a policy review covering the 14th Five-Year Plan period, the central bank said courts issued more than 2,000 money laundering judgments in 2025 under Article 191 of the Criminal Law. Officials said criminal groups are increasingly using virtual currencies, nomine
articles.content:
The People's Bank of China said authorities will increase enforcement against money laundering involving virtual currencies, underground banks and cross-border fund transfers as part of the country's anti-money laundering framework.

In a policy review covering the 14th Five-Year Plan period, the central bank said courts issued more than 2,000 money laundering judgments in 2025 under Article 191 of the Criminal Law. Officials said criminal groups are increasingly using virtual currencies, nominee accounts and other methods to move or conceal illicit funds.

The central bank said future work will focus on implementing the revised Anti-Money Laundering Law, expanding risk-based supervision, improving beneficial ownership reporting and increasing international enforcement cooperation. The statement follows other recent Chinese measures that classify crypto trading, token issuance and related financial products as illegal financial activities.

articles.summary:
Hong Kong's Securities and Futures Commission has added Aurum, also referred to as Aurum Foundation, to its alert list of suspicious virtual asset trading platforms. The regulator said the entity claims to be registered in Hong Kong and to offer virtual asset, futures and derivatives trading services through its website.

The SFC said Aurum does not hold a license from the commission and may be conducting regulated activities without authorization. The alert list is intended to help investors id
articles.content:
Hong Kong's Securities and Futures Commission has added Aurum, also referred to as Aurum Foundation, to its alert list of suspicious virtual asset trading platforms. The regulator said the entity claims to be registered in Hong Kong and to offer virtual asset, futures and derivatives trading services through its website.

The SFC said Aurum does not hold a license from the commission and may be conducting regulated activities without authorization. The alert list is intended to help investors identify platforms that may present risks.

Aurum is one of several entities the SFC has flagged in 2026 as Hong Kong expands oversight of digital asset activity. The regulator has advised investors to verify whether a platform appears on its official list of licensed virtual asset trading platforms before depositing funds.

articles.content:
Bitwise Chief Executive Hunter Horsley said the firm's XRP exchange-traded products in the United States and Europe have attracted more than $200 million in net inflows so far this year. He said the products also recorded inflows on June 23.

The figure refers to Bitwise's own XRP funds rather than the broader global market for XRP-linked exchange-traded products. According to the article, recent daily flow data showed additional net inflows during several June trading sessions.

The update follows earlier reports that XRP-related funds posted strong inflows in May, even as some Bitcoin and Ethereum products saw outflows. The products give investors regulated exposure to XRP through fund structures rather than direct token ownership.

articles.content:
Polymarket said June 23 that it has become the exclusive U.S. prediction market partner of Germany's Bundesliga. The company said official event contracts tied to the league and its clubs are now available on its platform.

The agreement adds to a broader set of partnerships Polymarket has announced across sports, entertainment and media, including arrangements involving UFC, Zuffa Boxing and the Golden Globes. The company has also said it is working with technology and data partners as it expands its products beyond crypto-focused users.

The announcement comes as Polymarket faces regulatory and public scrutiny. Recent reports cited in the article described questions about creator marketing practices, market integrity and whether some of the platform's offerings should be treated as sports betting under state law.

articles.summary:
Ripple said it has received preliminary approval in Luxembourg for a Crypto Asset Service Provider license under the European Union's Markets in Crypto-Assets framework. The approval, issued by Luxembourg's financial regulator, remains subject to final conditions.

If finalized, the license would allow Ripple to offer regulated crypto-asset services across the 30 countries of the European Economic Area through passporting rules. Ripple said the approval would work alongside its existing Electron
articles.content:
Ripple said it has received preliminary approval in Luxembourg for a Crypto Asset Service Provider license under the European Union's Markets in Crypto-Assets framework. The approval, issued by Luxembourg's financial regulator, remains subject to final conditions.

If finalized, the license would allow Ripple to offer regulated crypto-asset services across the 30 countries of the European Economic Area through passporting rules. Ripple said the approval would work alongside its existing Electronic Money Institution license in Europe and support payment services using crypto assets and stablecoins for banks, fintechs and corporate clients.

The company said it holds more than 75 regulatory licenses globally. The Luxembourg approval applies to Ripple's payments infrastructure and does not by itself change the rights of XRP holders.

articles.headline: Senate Approves Housing Bill Aimed at Affordability and Investor Limits
articles.deck: The Senate on Monday passed a housing bill that would limit large institutional purchases of single-family homes, expand federal support for local housing construction and chang...
articles.summary:
The Senate on Monday passed a housing bill that would limit large institutional purchases of single-family homes, expand federal support for local housing construction and change a federal rule affecting manufactured homes. articles.content:
The Senate on Monday passed a housing bill that would limit large institutional purchases of single-family homes, expand federal support for local housing construction and change a federal rule affecting manufactured homes. The measure passed 85-5 and now goes to the House, where lawmakers are expected to take it up next.

The bill, called the 21st Century Road to Housing Act, is a rare bipartisan housing measure and the first major federal housing legislation since the period following the 2008 financial crisis. Senators said the legislation is intended to address housing affordability and supply as home prices remain out of reach for many buyers.

One provision would bar institutional investors from buying more than 350 single-family homes. The bill would also expand grant programs for cities building new housing and remove a federal requirement that manufactured homes be built on a steel frame with wheels and an axle. According to the Niskanen Center, ending that rule could reduce the cost of building a manufactured home by as much as $10,000.

Housing researchers said some parts of the bill could help increase supply, while questioning how much effect the investor limit would have on prices. The Urban Institute has found that institutional investors own about 3 percent of single-family rentals and less than 0.5 percent of the total single-family housing stock. Laurie Goodman of the Urban Institute said removing the manufactured-housing chassis rule could lower costs, and David Garcia of the Terner Center for Housing Innovation said incentives for cities to build homes could have a meaningful effect on production.

Sharon Wilson Géno, president of the National Multifamily Housing Council, said the bill reflects broad agreement that housing affordability is a national issue, but said no single provision is likely to resolve the shortage on its own. Sen. Elizabeth Warren, a Massachusetts Democrat, said the federal government should play a larger role in reducing housing costs, while Sen. Tim Scott, a South Carolina Republican and chair of the Senate Banking Committee, said lawmakers in both parties could pursue additional housing measures in the future.

The House has not yet voted on the bill. Its next steps will help determine whether the measure becomes law and whether Congress takes up further housing legislation this year.
